pattern: Merging adjacent elements with AND is the same as choosing a contiguous partition of the array (segment count = n minus operations used); deciding the answer bit by bit from the top, greedily assuming a bit can be zero and checking feasibility, pins down the minimum achievable OR.
hints: Repeatedly AND-merging two neighbors is equivalent to picking a partition of the array into contiguous groups, each group collapsing to the AND of its members; using at most k operations means keeping at least n-k groups.

For a candidate bitmask x, a group is 'safe' when its AND has no bit outside x; greedily close a group the moment its running AND becomes safe -- that maximizes the number of groups you can form while staying inside x.

Decide the answer bit by bit from the highest bit down: tentatively leave the current bit at 0 (with every lower bit granted for free) and count the maximum groups achievable within at most k operations; if that's impossible even with all lower bits free, the bit must be forced to 1, and you carry that forced bit into every later check.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, only one operation available
two elements, one operation merges everything
all zeros already OR to zero no matter what
k allows merging the whole array into a single AND

stress.description: small random arrays with random k, exhaustively comparable to the oracle
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 12)))
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 31) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(0, n - 1) if n > 1 else 0
    return [nums, k]
stress.note: kept small so the exponential partition oracle stays fast

stress.description: larger random arrays with wider value range, no oracle involved at this size
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 20000))
    nums = [rng.randint(0, (1 << 30) - 1) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
    return [nums, k]
stress.note: exercises the bit-greedy solution at realistic scale
